name: speckit-pipeline
description: Full spec-first development pipeline: specify → plan → research → tasks → commit → implement (phase-by-phase with audit gates) → final audit → smoke test → PR. Mirrors the speckit.run skill using ruflo workflow orchestration.

agents.name: Specification Writer
agents.description: Creates feature specifications from PRD and user description. Produces spec.md with user stories, FRs, and acceptance criteria.

agents.name: Technical Planner
agents.description: Generates implementation plans, interface contracts, research docs, and data models from specifications.

agents.name: Dependency Resolver
agents.description: Finds and fetches external dependencies referenced in PRD and plan. Clones starters/templates to vendor/.

agents.name: Task Breakdown Generator
agents.description: Breaks plans into phased, dependency-aware task lists with FR references and file paths.

agents.name: Phase Implementer
agents.description: Implements one phase of tasks. Writes code matching contract signatures, adds FR comments, writes tests, marks tasks [X].

agents.name: Phase Auditor
agents.description: Verifies FR comments exist in code, signatures match contracts, tests reference acceptance scenarios. Fixes gaps or documents blockers.

agents.name: PRD Compliance Auditor
agents.description: Bidirectional audit: PRD→Spec (all requirements covered?) and Spec→Code→Test (all FRs implemented and tested?).

agents.name: Runtime Smoke Tester
agents.description: Scaffolds project in temp dir, builds it, runs it, verifies it works. Tests real runtime behavior, not just unit tests.

tasks.name: Step 1: Create Feature Specification
tasks.description: Read PRD and constitution, generate spec.md with user stories, FRs, and acceptance criteria.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are the specification writer for a spec-first development pipeline.

READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- ${prd_path} — the product requirements document
- ${constitution_path} — governing development principles
- ${spec_template} — the template format for specs

FEATURE TO SPECIFY:
${feature_description}

YOUR TASK:
1. Create a git branch named with a short 2-4 word slug from the feature description (e.g., 'feat/001-module-installer')
2. Create specs/<feature>/spec.md following the template exactly
3. Include:
   - User stories with Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios
   - Functional requirements with unique IDs (FR-001, FR-002, etc.)
   - Non-functional requirements
   - Success criteria that are measurable
   - Edge cases
4. Every FR must be testable and traceable to a PRD requirement

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT ask questions — the PRD has everything needed
- Do NOT implement anything — only produce the spec
- Do NOT invent requirements not in the PRD

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Branch name: <name>
- Spec path: <path>
- FR count: <number>
- User story count: <number>

tasks.name: Step 2: Generate Implementation Plan + Contracts
tasks.description: Read spec, generate plan.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/interfaces.md, quickstart.md.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are the technical planner for a spec-first development pipeline.

READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- ${constitution_path} — governing principles (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — the feature specification you are planning for
- ${plan_template} — template format for plans
- ${interfaces_template} — template format for interface contracts

YOUR TASK:
Generate these artifacts in specs/${feature_name}/:

1. plan.md — Technical implementation plan including:
   - Tech stack decisions with justification
   - Architecture overview
   - Implementation phases (Phase 1: Setup, Phase 2: Foundational, Phase 3+: User Stories)
   - File list with purpose for each file
   - Risk mitigation

2. research.md — Technology decisions, alternatives considered, rationale

3. data-model.md — Key entities, relationships, data flow

4. contracts/interfaces.md — MANDATORY. For EVERY exported function:
   - Exact function name
   - Parameters with types
   - Return type
   - sync vs async
   - Which FR it implements (// FR-NNN)
   This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for implementation.

5. quickstart.md — How to integrate/use the feature

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT ask questions
- Do NOT implement anything
- Every function in contracts/ must trace to an FR in spec.md
- No async unless justified. No any types. Concrete types only.
- Constitution principles are NON-NEGOTIABLE — if plan violates constitution, it's an ERROR

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Artifacts created: <list of file paths>
- Function count in contracts: <number>
- Phase count: <number>

tasks.name: Step 3: Resolve External Dependencies
tasks.description: Find and fetch every external dependency the PRD and plan reference. Clone starters to vendor/. This is the ONLY step that may surface questions.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are a research agent resolving external dependencies.

READ THESE FILES:
- ${prd_path} — look for named projects, frameworks, starters, libraries
- specs/${feature_name}/research.md — technology decisions from planning
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — the implementation plan
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — the feature specification

YOUR JOB:
Find and fetch every external project, starter, template, or library that the PRD and plan reference BY NAME. These are real projects on GitHub or npm — not things to build from scratch.

For each named external dependency:
1. SEARCH GitHub and the web to find the actual repository or package
2. If it's a starter/template repo (e.g., 'Tamagui Takeout', 'takeout-free', 'create-t3-app'):
   - Clone or download it into vendor/{name}/ in the project root
   - Document its file structure in specs/${feature_name}/research.md under '## External Dependencies'
   - Note which parts to use as base vs adapt vs ignore
3. If it's a library/package:
   - Verify correct package name and latest stable version
   - Document in research.md
4. If you CANNOT find or access a dependency:
   - Report as QUESTION: 'The PRD references {name} but I cannot find/access it. Please provide: (a) a URL/path, (b) confirm recreate from scratch, or (c) suggest alternative.'

UPDATE these files:
- specs/${feature_name}/research.md — add '## External Dependencies' section
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — update file references to point to vendor/ paths

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Dependencies resolved: <list with URLs>
- Dependencies cloned to vendor/: <list with paths>
- Questions for user: <list — ONLY if something cannot be found>

tasks.name: Step 4: Generate Task Breakdown
tasks.description: Break plan into phased, ordered task list with FR references, file paths, and parallelization markers.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are generating a task breakdown for implementation.

READ THESE FILES:
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — requirements source
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — technical architecture and phases
- specs/${feature_name}/research.md — technology decisions and external deps
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — function signatures (source of truth)
- ${tasks_template} — template format for tasks

YOUR TASK:
Create specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md with a complete task breakdown.

FORMAT (STRICT):
- [ ] T001 [P] [US1] Description with exact file path

Where:
- Checkbox is required
- T001, T002... are sequential task IDs
- [P] marks parallelizable tasks (no shared file dependencies)
- [US1], [US2]... link to user story phases
- Description includes the exact file path to create/modify

PHASE STRUCTURE:
- Phase 1: Setup (project init, dependencies, config)
- Phase 2: Foundational (blocking prerequisites, shared utilities)
- Phase 3+: One phase per user story from spec.md (P1 stories first, then P2, P3)
- Final Phase: Polish & cross-cutting (docs, lint, integration tests)

RULES:
- Every task must reference at least one FR from spec.md
- Tasks touching the same file CANNOT both be marked [P]
- Every function in contracts/interfaces.md must appear in at least one task
- Setup tasks come before tasks that depend on them

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Tasks path: <path>
- Total tasks: <number>
- Phase count: <number>
- Phases: <list of phase names>

tasks.name: Step 5: Commit Spec Artifacts
tasks.description: Git add and commit all spec artifacts and vendor dependencies before any implementation begins.
tasks.claudePrompt:
Commit all specification artifacts to git. This must happen BEFORE any implementation code is written.

Run these commands:
```bash
git add specs/${feature_name}/ CLAUDE.md
if [ -d vendor/ ]; then git add vendor/; fi
git commit -m 'Add spec artifacts for ${feature_name}'
```

VERIFY after commit:
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md exists in git
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md exists in git
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md exists in git
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md exists in git

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Commit hash: <hash>
- Files committed: <count>

tasks.name: Step 6a: Implement Phase 1 (Setup)
tasks.description: Implement ONLY Phase 1 tasks. Write code matching contracts, add FR comments, write tests, mark tasks [X], commit.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are implementing Phase 1 (Setup) of feature ${feature_name}.

CRITICAL — READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — find all tasks in Phase 1
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — all functions MUST match these signatures exactly
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — technical architecture
- specs/${feature_name}/research.md — check for external deps in vendor/
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — requirements source
- ${constitution_path} — governing principles

If vendor/ contains external starter templates or libraries referenced in plan.md, USE THEM as the base — do not recreate from scratch.

IMPLEMENT ONLY Phase 1 tasks. For each task:
1. Write code matching the contract signature EXACTLY
2. Add // FR-NNN comment referencing the spec requirement
3. Mark the task [X] in tasks.md IMMEDIATELY after completing it
4. Write tests that reference the acceptance scenario (// SC-NNN or // Scenario: ...)

RULES:
- Do NOT implement tasks from other phases
- Do NOT stop until every Phase 1 task is marked [X]
- If you hit an error, fix it and continue
- Every exported function MUST match contracts/interfaces.md
- No async unless the contract says async

When done:
1. Run tests for this phase
2. Commit with message: 'Implement Phase 1: Setup'

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Tasks completed: <count>/<total in phase>
- Tests: <passing>/<total>
- Files created/modified: <list>
- Commit hash: <hash>

tasks.name: Step 6b: Audit Phase 1
tasks.description: Verify Phase 1 implementation: FR comments, contract compliance, test coverage. Fix gaps or document blockers.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are auditing Phase 1 of feature ${feature_name}.

READ THESE FILES:
- ${prd_path} — source requirements
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — the FRs
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — function signatures
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — check that all Phase 1 tasks are marked [X]

For every FR that Phase 1 tasks claimed to implement:

1. VERIFY the implementation exists — read the actual source file, find the function, confirm it matches the contract signature
2. VERIFY the FR comment exists in the source code (// FR-NNN)
3. VERIFY a test exists that references the acceptance scenario
4. VERIFY the test actually tests the behavior (not just a stub)
5. For template/scaffold tasks: VERIFY generated files have real content, not placeholders
6. If the PRD references an external project: VERIFY the implementation uses it rather than recreating from scratch

If ANY gap is found:
- Try to FIX it (add missing comment, write missing test, flesh out stub)
- If unfixable, document in specs/${feature_name}/blockers.md

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Phase 1 compliance: X/Y FRs verified
- Gaps found and fixed: <list>
- Blockers: <list>
- Test results for this phase

tasks.name: Step 6a: Implement Phase 2 (Foundational)
tasks.description: Implement ONLY Phase 2 tasks. Same rules as Phase 1.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are implementing Phase 2 (Foundational) of feature ${feature_name}.

CRITICAL — READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — find all tasks in Phase 2
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — all functions MUST match these signatures exactly
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — technical architecture
- specs/${feature_name}/research.md — check for external deps in vendor/
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — requirements source
- ${constitution_path} — governing principles

If vendor/ contains external starter templates or libraries referenced in plan.md, USE THEM as the base — do not recreate from scratch.

IMPLEMENT ONLY Phase 2 tasks. For each task:
1. Write code matching the contract signature EXACTLY
2. Add // FR-NNN comment referencing the spec requirement
3. Mark the task [X] in tasks.md IMMEDIATELY after completing it
4. Write tests that reference the acceptance scenario

RULES:
- Do NOT implement tasks from other phases
- Do NOT stop until every Phase 2 task is marked [X]
- If you hit an error, fix it and continue
- Every exported function MUST match contracts/interfaces.md

When done:
1. Run tests for this phase
2. Commit with message: 'Implement Phase 2: Foundational'

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Tasks completed: <count>/<total in phase>
- Tests: <passing>/<total>
- Files created/modified: <list>
- Commit hash: <hash>

tasks.name: Step 6b: Audit Phase 2
tasks.description: Verify Phase 2 implementation. Same checks as Phase 1 audit.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are auditing Phase 2 of feature ${feature_name}.

READ THESE FILES:
- ${prd_path} — source requirements
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — the FRs
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — function signatures
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — check that all Phase 2 tasks are marked [X]

For every FR that Phase 2 tasks claimed to implement:

1. VERIFY the implementation exists — read the actual source file, find the function, confirm it matches the contract signature
2. VERIFY the FR comment exists in the source code (// FR-NNN)
3. VERIFY a test exists that references the acceptance scenario
4. VERIFY the test actually tests the behavior (not just a stub)
5. For template/scaffold tasks: VERIFY generated files have real content, not placeholders
6. If the PRD references an external project: VERIFY the implementation uses it rather than recreating from scratch

If ANY gap is found:
- Try to FIX it (add missing comment, write missing test, flesh out stub)
- If unfixable, document in specs/${feature_name}/blockers.md

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Phase 2 compliance: X/Y FRs verified
- Gaps found and fixed: <list>
- Blockers: <list>
- Test results for this phase

tasks.name: Step 6a: Implement Phase 3 (User Stories)
tasks.description: Implement ONLY Phase 3 tasks (first user story batch).
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are implementing Phase 3 (User Stories) of feature ${feature_name}.

CRITICAL — READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — find all tasks in Phase 3
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — all functions MUST match these signatures exactly
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — technical architecture
- specs/${feature_name}/research.md — check for external deps in vendor/
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — requirements source
- ${constitution_path} — governing principles

If vendor/ contains external starter templates or libraries referenced in plan.md, USE THEM as the base — do not recreate from scratch.

IMPLEMENT ONLY Phase 3 tasks. For each task:
1. Write code matching the contract signature EXACTLY
2. Add // FR-NNN comment referencing the spec requirement
3. Mark the task [X] in tasks.md IMMEDIATELY after completing it
4. Write tests that reference the acceptance scenario

RULES:
- Do NOT implement tasks from other phases
- Do NOT stop until every Phase 3 task is marked [X]
- If you hit an error, fix it and continue

When done:
1. Run tests for this phase
2. Commit with message: 'Implement Phase 3: User Stories'

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Tasks completed: <count>/<total in phase>
- Tests: <passing>/<total>
- Files created/modified: <list>
- Commit hash: <hash>

tasks.name: Step 6b: Audit Phase 3
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are auditing Phase 3 of feature ${feature_name}.

READ THESE FILES:
- ${prd_path} — source requirements
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — the FRs
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — function signatures
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — check that all Phase 3 tasks are marked [X]

For every FR that Phase 3 tasks claimed to implement:

1. VERIFY the implementation exists — read the actual source file, find the function, confirm it matches the contract signature
2. VERIFY the FR comment exists in the source code (// FR-NNN)
3. VERIFY a test exists that references the acceptance scenario
4. VERIFY the test actually tests the behavior (not just a stub)
5. For template/scaffold tasks: VERIFY generated files have real content, not placeholders

If ANY gap is found:
- Try to FIX it or document in specs/${feature_name}/blockers.md

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Phase 3 compliance: X/Y FRs verified
- Gaps found and fixed: <list>
- Blockers: <list>

tasks.name: Step 6a: Implement Phase 4 (Polish)
tasks.description: Implement final phase tasks (docs, lint, integration tests, cross-cutting concerns).
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are implementing Phase 4 (Polish & Cross-cutting) of feature ${feature_name}.

READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — find all tasks in Phase 4 (and any remaining phases)
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — all functions MUST match these signatures
- specs/${feature_name}/plan.md — technical architecture
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — requirements source
- ${constitution_path} — governing principles

IMPLEMENT all remaining tasks (Phase 4 and any phases beyond). For each task:
1. Write code matching contract signatures exactly
2. Add // FR-NNN comments
3. Mark each task [X] immediately
4. Write any remaining tests

This is the FINAL implementation phase. When done:
1. Run the FULL test suite (not just this phase)
2. Verify all tasks in tasks.md are marked [X]
3. Commit with message: 'Implement Phase 4: Polish & cross-cutting'

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Tasks completed: <count>/<total remaining>
- All tasks done: yes/no
- Full test suite: <passing>/<total>
- Files created/modified: <list>
- Commit hash: <hash>

tasks.name: Step 6b: Audit Phase 4
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are auditing Phase 4 (and any remaining phases) of feature ${feature_name}.

READ THESE FILES:
- ${prd_path} — source requirements
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — the FRs
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — function signatures
- specs/${feature_name}/tasks.md — ALL tasks should now be marked [X]

Verify ALL remaining FRs from Phase 4+:
1. VERIFY implementation exists and matches contract
2. VERIFY FR comments in source
3. VERIFY tests exist and reference acceptance scenarios
4. VERIFY tests are real (not stubs)

Also check: are there ANY tasks still marked [ ] in tasks.md? If so, flag them.

Fix gaps or document in specs/${feature_name}/blockers.md.

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Phase 4 compliance: X/Y FRs verified
- Uncompleted tasks: <list or 'none'>
- Gaps found and fixed: <list>
- Blockers: <list>

tasks.name: Step 7: Full PRD Compliance Audit
tasks.description: Bidirectional audit: PRD→Spec and Spec→Code→Test. Fix gaps or document blockers. Report compliance %.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are running the FINAL PRD compliance audit for feature ${feature_name}.

READ THESE FILES:
- ${prd_path} — extract EVERY functional requirement, deliverable, and user story
- specs/${feature_name}/spec.md — extract every FR-NNN
- specs/${feature_name}/contracts/interfaces.md — every function signature
- ${constitution_path} — governing principles

Check BOTH directions:

Phase A — PRD → Spec:
Does every PRD requirement have at least one covering FR in spec.md?
List any PRD requirements with NO covering FR.

Phase B — Spec → Code → Test:
For every FR-NNN:
1. Search source files for // FR-NNN comment — READ the actual function, not just the filename
2. Verify the function signature matches contracts/interfaces.md
3. Search test files for acceptance scenario references
4. Verify tests actually test the behavior (not stubs)
5. For templates/scaffolds: verify files contain real, functional content — not placeholder stubs

For generated project templates specifically:
- Does the README exist with setup instructions?
- Do template files contain real, runnable code?
- Would a generated project actually install dependencies and start?
- If the PRD names a specific starter/template, is the actual project used — not recreated from scratch?

Fix gaps or document blockers in specs/${feature_name}/blockers.md.

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Overall compliance: X/Y FRs (percentage)
- PRD→Spec gaps: <list or 'none'>
- Spec→Code gaps: <list or 'none'>
- Spec→Test gaps: <list or 'none'>
- Blockers: <list or 'none'>
- PASS or FAIL

tasks.name: Step 8: Runtime Smoke Test
tasks.description: Build the project, run it in a temp dir, verify it actually works at runtime.
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are smoke testing feature ${feature_name}.

Read specs/${feature_name}/plan.md to determine the project type (CLI, web app, mobile, API).

Perform a full runtime smoke test:

1. Create a temp directory
2. Build the project (bun run build or equivalent)
3. Run the primary command or start the server
4. Verify it actually works:
   - CLI: run the main commands, check exit codes and output
   - Web: start dev server, curl the URL, verify 200 response with real content
   - Mobile: verify prebuild succeeds
   - API: hit health and primary endpoints

For CLI projects that generate other projects:
   - Run the create command to generate a project
   - cd into the generated project
   - Run bun install (or equivalent)
   - Verify it installs without errors
   - Try to start the dev server
   - Verify basic functionality works

Report PASS/FAIL for each check with exact error output on failure.
Clean up temp dir (keep on failure for debugging).

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- Project type: <type>
- Checks run: <list>
- Results: PASS/FAIL per check
- Overall: PASS or FAIL
- Error output (if any): <details>

tasks.name: Step 9: Create Pull Request
tasks.description: Push branch and create PR with summary, stats, compliance, and smoke test results.
tasks.claudePrompt:
Create a pull request for feature ${feature_name}.

1. Check for branch conflicts:
   git ls-remote --heads origin ${branch_name}
   If exists, append -v2, -v3 etc. and rename: git branch -m <new-name>

2. Push: git push -u origin ${branch_name}

3. Create PR:
   gh pr create --title '<short summary under 70 chars>' --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
   ## Summary
   - <bullet points of what was built>

   ## Stats
   - **Files**: <count>
   - **Tests**: <count> passing
   - **Compliance**: <percentage from final audit>
   - **Smoke Test**: <PASS/FAIL>

   ## Test plan
   - [x] Unit tests passing
   - [x] Coverage >= 80%
   - [x] PRD audit completed
   - [x] Smoke test completed

   Built with ruflo workflow pipeline
   EOF
   )"

4. Report the PR URL.

OUTPUT — report exactly:
- PR URL: <url>
- Branch: <name>
